name: Nut Brittle
recipeInstructions:
1. Generously grease two 15-1/2 x 10-1/2 x 1 inch jelly-roll pans.
2. Combine corn syrup, sugar, and water in a medium-sized heavy saucepan. Stir to blend.
3. Bring to boiling over medium-high heat, stirring to dissolve sugar.
4. Lower the heat to simmer; continue cooking without stirring until the mixture registers 250 degrees on a candy thermometer (about 10 minutes).
5. Stir in nuts. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the mixture registers 300 degrees. Remove from heat.
6. Carefully stir in butter, salt, baking soda, and vanilla.
7. Working quickly, pour the mixture onto the prepared jelly-roll pans and spread as thinly as possible.
8. Cool pans of brittle completely on wire racks.
9. Break brittle into rough bite-size pieces. It may be necessary to loosen some of the brittle from the bottom of the pan with a knife before breaking.
10. Store tightly covered.
Makes 2 pounds.
